From those thirteen iris-shaped panes of glass, thirteen different colors of cool light touch the chamber below, illuminating with a pale grace.

node.name: The Iris Rim, Shrine of Destruction
node.description: The walls of this room are formed of smooth black trachyte, the reflective surface carved with images of Gorbesh soldiers ransacking and pillaging the former temple. Resting in the center of the shrine, a dull iron altar leans awkwardly, its base cracked and broken. A fierce depiction of the power of destruction, this room is an eerie reminder that nothing is eternal.

node.name: The Iris Rim, Shrine of Outwardness
node.description: Dedicated to spirit manifested outwardly, one wall of this room is dominated by a rich tapestry. Its opulent stitchery depicts a kneeling, plate-clad warrior humbly offering his blade to an Alfar whose blazing, unearthly beauty surrounds him with radiance. A stained glass roundel depicting Elanthia graces the ceiling, traced in gold with the major trade routes of land and sea. A bright flicker in one corner reveals an extraordinary sculpture -- a crystal phoenix rising amidst real flames.

node.name: The Iris Rim, Warrior's Shrine
node.description: This room is faced with polished deep red porphyry and is dedicated to those whose burden in life is blood and battle. Burning steadily, two torches frame a small steel altar forged from broken weapons, and cast long finger-like shadows across the shards of glistening metal. A simple porcelain bowl rests in the middle of the altar, its white surface stained copper by the many offerings of blood droplets the temple monks provide during their vigils.

node.name: The Iris Rim, Shrine of Thought
node.description: To free the mind for journeying along the pathways of thought, this room is bare of ornamentation, the bustle and din of the outside world held at bay by thick marble walls draped in blue-black velvet. Deep silence reigns here, and soft darkness surrounds all. Suspended near the apex of the high arched ceiling is a luminous crystalline sphere, its silvery light falling upon a single white lotus blossom centered on a little sandalwood altar.

node.name: The Iris Rim, Shrine of Healing
node.description: Designed to honor those who devote their lives to the salvation of bodies and souls, this shrine is decorated with a profusion of flowers and medicinal herbs. Fresh flowers adorn the wall next to the alabaster arch leading into the hallway, and a collage of dried flowers protected by glass looms over the altar in the back of the room.

node.name: The Iris Rim, Shrine of Foresight
node.description: This shrine is possessed of a dreamlike ambiance, watery illumination emanating from the core of a delicate crystal bowl resting upon the central altar. Faint streaks of colored light meet walls obscured by sheer curtains of smoke and shadow around the circumference of the room painted constellations and moons tease at the edge of recognition before again slipping behind the cloak of satin darkness.

node.name: Ve Aevan Gaenan, Southwest Stairway
node.description: Rows of mahogany benches blend into a continuous sea of darkly stained wood, the wedge-shaped seating area bisected by the clean lines of these granite steps. Further up the stairs, a thick metallic pillar stands firmly, rooted deep into the foundation as if it had been built around rather than added to the structure.

node.name: Ve Aevan Gaenan, The Pillar
node.description: Rising from deep within the pale granite, a steel-like pillar stands with regal dignity, its surface carefully etched with the horned owl of Firulf and the great wolf of Meraud. Its composition strikingly similar to the altar below, the formidable column is capped with a halved sphere of an opalescent mineral whose milky interior swirls with a tamed power. Spacious seating areas rest on either side of this great marker, each perched in silent vigil high above the sacred altar.

node.name: Ve Aevan Gaenan, Southwest Stairway
node.description: From this landing poised just beneath the Iris Rim, a sweeping granite stairway climbs onto the annular ledge above, providing access to the six arches that line the equator of this spherical space, and drawing one toward the watchful Eyes of the Thirteen. The stairs continue downward in a gentle curve toward the metallic altar, their progress as fluid and graceful as water streaming down a rough face of rock.

node.name: Ve Aevan Gaenan, Southeast Stairway
node.description: Individual rows of darkly stained benches blend into a continuous sea of mahogany, the wedge-shaped seating area bisected by the sharp lines of these pale steps. A thick metallic pillar stands further up the stairs, buried deep into the foundation as if it had been present far before the stone that currently surrounds it.

node.name: Ve Aevan Gaenan, The Pillar
node.description: From deep within these granite steps a great pillar rises proudly -- its surface skillfully etched with the unicorn of Hodierna and the great wolf of Meraud, its strength unassailable and without equal. A halved sphere of an opalescent mineral caps the pillar, its milky interior swirling with a lazy power. Spacious seating areas are perched on either side of this great marker, resting in silent vigil high about the sacred altar.

node.name: Ve Aevan Gaenan, Southeast Stairway
node.description: From this landing poised just beneath the Iris Rim, a sweeping granite stairway ascends to the circular ledge above, providing access to the six arches that line the equator of this spherical space, and drawing one toward the watchful Eyes of the Thirteen. The stairs continue downward in a gentle curve toward the metallic altar, their progress as fluid and graceful as water streaming down a rough face of rock.

node.name: Temple Grounds, Grand Stairway
node.description: Below to either side, paths curve around in between the main temple and the east and west towers. The monoliths stretch upwards and lean slightly toward the temple itself, as if grasping out toward the beacon area atop the structure, above the Eyes of The Thirteen. Smoothly cut granite steps lead upward to the north, climbing toward the massive temple.

node.name: Temple Grounds, Grand Stairway
node.description: The grand stairway continues its ascent to the north, approaching the looming orb of the temple. Ornate silverwillow banisters curve down from the entrance, polished to a lustrous sheen and carved to resemble flowing streams, trickling serenely to the bottom of the stairs.

node.name: Grand Stairway, Temple Entrance
node.description: The stairs descend to the south providing a spectacular view of the meandering Segoltha river. The grand orb-like temple yawns open directly to the north, its massive double doors sure to ward off any foolish invaders who may besiege this religious fortress. Two massive bronze sconces protrude above the entryway, each shaped like an outstretched feline's paw clutching an eternal flame -- surely a blessing from the goddess of civilization who aided in the reconstruction of this monument.

node.name: Wedding Glade, Outdoor Chapel
node.description: A smooth cobblestone path meanders its way from under a whitewashed latticework archway to the southeast, leading beneath the gentle shade of a gazebo to the north. Lush green modwyn vines reach up from the ground, coiling about the gazebo's posts to intertwine atop it, creating a picturesque canopy to shield any would-be wedding parties from the elements. Festive streamers of a myriad of colors weave a sweeping rainbow before the small chapel's entrance.

node.name: Outdoor Wedding Chapel, Reception Plaza
node.description: A thick carpet of pale golden-brown leaves covers the ground of this small plaza, barely allowing one to see the concealed cobbled road beneath. Festive golden streamers lazily dance about, tied to small wooden posts that hold the pruned hedges at bay. A large nearly-naked cherry tree rises in the center of the plaza, shedding its crisp golden feathers, having borne its children for the year.

The fresh, wintry crunch of the crisp white snow beneath each footstep heralds the arrival of the late months. A large cherry tree stands dormant, barren of leaves and sporting only a few small, frozen buds. The dulled daylight shines off the snow, casting an ambient glow upon everything in sight. The hedges appear as snowbanks, buried beneath the frosty white carpet that drapes over all in this small cul-de-sac.

node.name: Beneath the Wedding Glade, Sundering Chapel
node.description: Fresh dirt walls have been packed in crudely, a thin tunnel in the ceiling leading up to the wedding chapel above. The faint laughter of a joyous party is heard in the distance, muffled and given an eerie quality tinged with sorrow in this small hovel within the earth. A crude obsidian slab has been smoothed into a makeshift altar, littered with shattered crystal shards -- the remnants of former scission blades, and the only physical trace of the sundered wedding vows made by countless people before.

node.name: Temple, North Tower
node.description: Diffuse golden light falls from somewhere above, its honeyed beams drifting down the length of the tower. Matched flagstones of deep grey slate create a broad landing that punctuates the staircase as it continues its gradual climb.

node.name: Reception Hall, Stage
node.description: Smooth walnut boards underfoot and rich blue velvet curtains along the walls make this an elegant nook where minstrels and performers can share their music with those in the ballroom. Wooden chairs sit in neat rows facing the north, each with a silver stand set in front shaped like a lyre. A cleared space made in front of them is framed by multicolored silk streamers that flutter onto the stage.

node.name: Eye of Urrem'tier, Warding Chamber
node.description: Concave walls of pitch black obsidian carve out a small spherical chamber. Etched into the walls are strange sigils and warding runes that glimmer every so often with a white radiance. The chamber is bare save for a simple black ironwood stand in the center and an ebony rack on one wall.
